title: Horadric Cube — recipe book UI and expanded recipes
problem: The cube has 7 recipes discoverable only by trial-and-error — there is no in-game recipe book or Codex entry. Players have no way to learn recipes without external documentation. Additionally the recipe set is too small to support late-game crafting depth.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred UX + scope: recipe discovery UI was explicitly cut from the initial cube implementation

analysis: The Horadric Cube has 8 recipes but no in-game reference. Players using the cube had to guess or remember recipes from session to session. The Codex (Field Guide) is the natural home for a recipe reference since it already contains combat, items, and run tabs.
implementation: Added a 'crafting' tab (label: CUBE) to CodexScreen in src/screens/CodexScreen.tsx. Updated the Tab type union and TAB_LABELS record. Imported RECIPES and RecipeDef from src/data/recipes.ts. The crafting tab section maps over RECIPES, rendering a styled card per recipe with name, description, and formatted input requirements. Added a NoteCard tips section at the bottom with crafting guidance. Added recipeCard, recipeName, recipeDesc, recipeInputRow, recipeLabel, and recipeInput styles to the StyleSheet.

decisions.decision: Tab label is CUBE not CRAFTING to keep tab bar labels short and thematic
decisions.rationale: The tab bar is a horizontal scroll and the word CRAFTING at 8 chars pushes other tabs off screen on small phones. CUBE is instantly recognisable.

decisions.decision: Recipe inputs are rendered as an inline formatted string, not a table
decisions.rationale: Recipes have variable numbers of heterogeneous inputs; a table would require dynamic column counts and is harder to scan than a flat + delimited summary.

decisions.decision: RECIPES array is iterated directly from data/recipes.ts — no separate codex data layer
decisions.rationale: Recipes are the single source of truth. Duplicating them into a codex data file would create a drift risk.

successCriteria: CUBE tab appears in Codex tab bar. All 8 recipes render with name, description, and input summary. Tips NoteCard renders below. TypeScript compiles without errors.
knownLimitations: Recipe input formatting uses a simple string concat — complex nested inputs may look awkward if new recipe types are added
No 'try this recipe' shortcut to the Cube screen from the Codex
userGuide: Open the Field Guide from the main menu or map screen. Tap the CUBE tab to browse all Horadric Cube recipes — each card shows the recipe name, what it does, and what items you need to put in.
implementationNotes: RecipeDef type imported alongside RECIPES to satisfy TypeScript in the map callback
Input formatting handles all 5 RecipeInputType variants (rune, quality, potion, gem, any)

codeSnippets.label: CUBE tab type and label
Recipe card render
codeSnippets.snippet:
{RECIPES.map((recipe: RecipeDef) => (
  <View key={recipe.id} style={styles.recipeCard}>
    <Text style={styles.recipeName}>{recipe.name.toUpperCase()}</Text>
    <Text style={styles.recipeDesc}>{recipe.description}</Text>
    <View style={styles.recipeInputRow}>
      <Text style={styles.recipeLabel}>INPUT</Text>
      <Text style={styles.recipeInput}>
        {recipe.inputs.map(inp => {
          if (inp.type === 'rune')    return `${inp.count}\u00d7 rune (${inp.runeId})`
          if (inp.type === 'quality') return `${inp.count}\u00d7 ${inp.quality}${inp.slot ? ` ${inp.slot}` : ''}`
          if (inp.type === 'potion')  return `${inp.count}\u00d7 ${inp.potionId.replace(/_/g, ' ')}`
          if (inp.type === 'gem')     return `${inp.count}\u00d7 gem (${inp.gemId})`
          return `${inp.count}\u00d7 any`
        }).join('  +  ')}
      </Text>
    </View>
  </View>
))}
